subject: Geology
title: Early Warning Systems
description: A short lesson to help you apply Early Warning Systems.

hook.text: Your phone buzzes seconds before the shaking arrives. P-waves travel faster than the destructive S-waves behind them.

buildup.text: Early warning systems detect P-waves and send alerts before the slower, damaging S-waves and surface waves arrive.

discovery.text: Japan's system gave Tokyo 80 seconds of warning before the 2011 Tohoku shaking hit. Trains stopped automatically.

twist.text: Near the epicenter, warning time can be zero. The system works best for cities far enough away to receive the alert.

climax.text: Early warning systems save lives by stopping trains, opening fire station doors, and triggering shelter responses.

punchline.text: Seconds of warning can save thousands of lives.

quiz.question: How do earthquake early warning systems work?
options: They predict earthquakes days in advance
They detect fast P-waves and alert before slower damaging waves arrive
They use satellite images of faults
